notes: No pre-existing GB200 MiniMax M3 config, run, log, artifact, or metric; perf-changelog.yaml untouched.
Active research resumed after explicit GO; three-minute heartbeat started.

decision: Use reciprocal p90 TPOT for the required tail-interactivity frontier; stored p90_intvty is not equivalent because reciprocal reverses percentile order.

checks: join drain and no active-join leakage
notes: No runtime claim yet; first TP4 1P/1D c1 smoke remains required.
No node excluded; cluster fully idle before first dispatch.
DAG fanout can transiently make wire in-flight exceed one while the single root lane remains bounded; no runaway session concurrency observed.

decision: Probe native MultiConnector with NixlConnector first and SimpleCPUOffloadConnector second; never exceed 192 GiB per worker on this allocation.
Use 1/p90_tpot for the required p90-tail interactivity frontier and report p90_intvty only as a schema caveat.

notes: All accelerated-warmup wire requests returned before profiling; paused DAG work persisted, including one rootless lane holding a lane credit. This is the required no-leak drain/handoff behavior.
All 27 exported records are profiling-only. Peak root-session slots equaled target concurrency 1; wire fanout peaked at 3 without session leakage.

cause: MiniMax shared-FS INFMAX_WORKSPACE retained aggregate and checkpoint JSON outside GITHUB_WORKSPACE, so the workflow found 0/1 expected results after Slurm completed successfully.
fix: Sync matching aggregate and checkpoint files from INFMAX_WORKSPACE back to GITHUB_WORKSPACE in launch_gb200-nv.sh before workflow validation.

purpose: Logarithmic topology and concurrency search; validate shared-workspace artifact fix.

notes: All three two-node jobs started immediately on distinct nodes.

cause: All DP4/EP4 ranks fail engine initialization with ValueError: No MXFP8 MoE backends available.
decision: Exclude TP1+DP-attention+EP for MiniMax M3 MXFP8 on vLLM 04c2a8de; retain both nodes because evidence is a deterministic kernel/topology incompatibility, not node failure.

notes: Directional only because c1 completed 6-7 long-context requests in 300 seconds. Both topologies remain non-dominated across the two required objectives pending higher-concurrency points and final 900-second confirmation.

pareto_observation: TEP4 dominates TP4 for throughput versus p90 E2E at c4; both remain non-dominated for throughput versus required tail interactivity because TP4 trades 1.3% throughput for 22.1% higher interactivity.
cooldown_observation: The intentionally short 300-second probe reached the scenario cutoff with active DAG joins. Final candidates require >=900 seconds; cancelled records are excluded from metrics.

cause: After all 94 TEP4 c4 credits returned, cancellation drain still waited on an unset event and logged a false timeout with Need 0 more credits.
fix: Set all_credits_returned_event immediately when outstanding credits are zero after cancel_all_credits.
validation: 46 phase-runner unit tests and Ruff passed.

cause: JSONL shutdown waited on the periodic background task together with finite flush tasks, causing a false Timeout waiting for 0 pending flush tasks warning and risking cancellation of unrelated tasks.
fix: Track finite flush tasks separately and wait only for them before the final synchronous buffer flush.
validation: 54 targeted unit tests and Ruff passed.

pareto_observation: TP4 dominates TEP4 at c8 across throughput, p90 E2E, and required tail interactivity. The low GPU-cache hit makes TP4 c8 the selected CPU-offload stress point.

c12 is beyond the throughput/E2E knee: severe prefill queuing collapses throughput and drives p90 TTFT above 113 seconds. TP4 c12 remains a candidate only for the separately defined throughput-versus-1/p90-TPOT frontier.

memory_rationale: Measured prefill baseline is 460 GiB against 840 GiB allocatable host DRAM. The 192 GiB ceiling leaves at least 188 GiB allocation headroom; swap is zero. MultiConnector orders NixlConnector before lazy SimpleCPUOffloadConnector so disaggregated transfer remains primary.
guardrail: Launcher rejects any CPU_OFFLOAD_GIB outside 64, 128, or 192.

cause: vLLM MultiConnector and SimpleCPUOffloadConnector initialize correctly, but Dynamo 1.3.0.dev20260618 rejects top-level kv_connector=MultiConnector in PD request handling; its registry supports only NixlConnector and MooncakeConnector.
fix: Patch Dynamo's container-local PD protocol registry during setup so MultiConnector uses NixlConnectorProtocol. This preserves NIXL as the first MultiConnector child while SimpleCPUOffloadConnector remains a local fallback tier.

finding: srt runs the recipe setup script before installing the pinned Dynamo wheel, so the initial bridge location was too early and was not rerun.
fix: Patch srtctl runtime_scripts/dynamo_wheels.py in the staged clone so it updates Dynamo's installed protocol registry immediately after wheel installation and before the worker process starts.
validation: Applied the exact launcher-generated transform to the pinned de59739b runtime script; the transformed installer compiles and the generated 64 GiB recipe contains the expected MultiConnector configuration.
No BranchOrchestrator leak warning, no zero-credit drain timeout, no pending-flush timeout; aggregate and 300 profiling-only records exported.

connector_evidence: SimpleCPUOffloadWorker allocated 4,481/8,962/13,443 blocks per rank for 64/128/192 GiB logical-worker tiers. MultiConnector does not expose SimpleCPU transfer counters through the current vLLM Prometheus schema; logs show CPU-hit state and stale-pin self-release warnings on 128/192.
decision: Exclude 64 GiB as dominated. Retain 128 and 192 GiB as non-dominated latency/interactivity tradeoffs versus the 5,478 tok/s/GPU no-offload control.

rationale: c2/c6/c10 refine logarithmic coarse points. TEP is retained only below its c8 domination point. The 120-second grace captures long responses that started before the 900-second cutoff and prevents connector cancellation cleanup from biasing metrics.

reason: User removed all TEP/expert-parallel prefill candidates from GB200 acceptance scope.
preserved_evidence: GitHub artifact metadata and completed offload192 artifacts retained under run 28184509953; durable metadata copied to /tmp/inferencex-agent-watch/gb200-obsolete-28184509953-evidence.

rationale: Remove all GB200 TEP/expert-parallel prefill candidates; retain TP4 prefill variants only.
affected_points: Removed TEP c1/c2/c4/c6; TP4 and offload points unchanged.

rationale: Pin canonical combined AIPerf containing cancellation-drain, pending-flush, api-time overlap, and accelerated-warmup fixes.
Make the required 60-minute measurement duration a source-config invariant; workflow also passes duration-override=3600.
affected_points: All 13 TP4-prefill points; warmup remains 600 seconds and grace remains 120 seconds.

cause: Wrong top-level workflow ref used main's incompatible multi-node AgentX array inputs; no matrix jobs materialized and collect-agentic-results failed with no artifacts.
fix: Dispatch from required coordinator/minimaxm3-agentx-autoresearch workflow ref; do not rerun this run.

cause: All decode TP ranks hit NIXL handshake_failed/handshake_setup_failed with zmq.error.Again; no completed records for most of the profile window, so the apparent throughput is cooldown-biased.
decision: Exclude c6-192 from both Pareto frontiers. Do not exclude nodes; the server drained, re-handshook, and the same job completed c8/c10 successfully.

validation: All 13 profile exports and aggregate JSON files exist. No ProfileAborted, failed_request_threshold, replay_rc!=0, or missing profile export. GitHub green is not accepted for the two shortened points.

rationale: Propagate nonzero AgentX replay/launcher status and reject missing or invalid agentic outputs without overwriting GB200 configuration changes.
validation: Commit is branch HEAD ancestor before new probes; GB200 config and AIPerf gitlink remain unchanged.

outcome: mandatory hold satisfied before database access

finding: Ingestion began after the mandatory hold and was still attaching trace blobs. All seven rows were labeled offload_mode=off, while artifact metrics show the c6 row is the invalid offload192 result; offload variant identity is therefore not trustworthy in this snapshot.
decision: Do not treat absent rows as final missing ingestion yet. Use per-variant GitHub artifacts as the authoritative 13-point source and re-query Neon after focused probes.

caveat: Different model, precision, and serving topology make this a directional capacity check, not an apples-to-apples model comparison.
decision: Current GB200 search space fails the required aggregate-capacity bar; scale TP4 prefill/decode worker allocation and retune concurrency.

findings: No-offload c10 is the throughput knee at 37.4k tok/s aggregate; c12 collapses to 21.6k with p90 TTFT 155s. All valid 128/192-GiB points lose to no-offload c6 or higher on both required frontiers. Holes remain around c5/c7/c9 and in worker allocation above 1P1D.
decision: Prune offload tiers and c12 from the improved full sweep after scaled topology probes; retain topology diversity until scaling evidence is complete.

constraints: Prefill and decode are TP4 EP1; no TEP/DEP/expert prefill; no max-model-len; prefix caching and chunked-prefill default retained; no CPU offload because both tested tiers are dominated over 3600 seconds.
rationale: Separate scheduler-budget effects from decode scaling, then add prefill capacity if 1P3D becomes prefill-bound.

comparison: 8.2% higher aggregate throughput than valid 1P1D c8, but 27.9% lower per-GPU throughput; dominates 1P1D c10 on aggregate throughput, p90 E2E, and required tail interactivity.
decision: Keep 1P2D and continue c12/c16 to locate its knee.

diagnosis: One prefill worker queued ten requests while three decode workers remained mostly idle; throughput and tails are dominated by lower-GPU topologies.
decision: Prune 1P3D c12/c16/c20. Cancel only this new experiment after preserving c12 artifacts so its runner can execute 2P3D.

cause: Evidence-backed prefill saturation and complete Pareto domination at c12; higher concurrency cannot repair the 1P:3D worker imbalance.
outcome: GHA job failed as intended through exit-propagation commit 999adab3; nodes remain healthy and are not excluded.

comparison: Default 8192/64 c6 has 34.95k aggregate, 55.03s p90 E2E, and 1.30s p90 TTFT. The larger budget loses 10.4% aggregate and worsens latency, with only a small interactivity tradeoff.
decision: Retain through c8 only to test whether the larger budget shifts the higher-concurrency knee.

finding: The unique result key retained one row per concurrency because all three no-offload/128-GiB/192-GiB variants emitted offload_mode=off. Final c6/c8/c10 rows contain the offload192 artifacts, including the invalid 451.88-second c6 run; no-offload and offload128 rows were overwritten.
impact: Neon cannot represent the six distinct offload points from this run, so database ingestion is incomplete despite all 13 GitHub artifacts. Official Pareto and pruning use variant-qualified artifacts, not these collided rows.
mitigation: Improved final search space removes dominated CPU-offload variants, eliminating this collision for the accepted sweep.

validation: Two TP4 EP1 prefill workers and three TP4 EP1 decode workers registered; no max-model-len and no startup failure.

diagnosis: c12 is beyond the 1P2D knee and becomes prefill-limited; it is dominated by c8 and lower-GPU points.
decision: Keep 1P2D c8 only; cancel c16 after preserving c12.

cause: c12 throughput collapsed 50.9% versus c8 and p90 TTFT reached 151.2s; c16 is safely dominated.
outcome: GHA job expected to fail through exit propagation; nodes remain healthy and are not excluded.

comparison: Default 8192/64 c8 is strictly better: 36.71k aggregate, 56.83s p90 E2E, 41.67 tok/s tail interactivity, and 1.35s p90 TTFT.
decision: Reject 16384-token/128-seq scheduler budget; cancel c10 after preserving c8.

cause: The larger scheduler budget is Pareto-dominated at both c6 and c8; c10 cannot recover the cache collapse and latency tails.
outcome: GHA job expected to fail through exit propagation; nodes remain healthy and are not excluded.

comparison: 2.45x the best valid 1P1D aggregate throughput with nearly unchanged per-GPU throughput, but only 79.0% of the directional 116.1k B200 aggregate capacity bar and with an 84.4s p90 TTFT tail.
decision: Continue c20/c24 for throughput saturation; separately probe balanced 3P4D c12/c16/c20 to add capacity without relying only on higher concurrency.

constraints: No TEP/DEP/expert prefill and no max-model-len.
rationale: At 28 GPUs, preserving the measured 2P3D c16 per-GPU rate would yield about 128.4k aggregate tok/s. c12 tests the lower-tail point; c16 tests the capacity target; c20 locates saturation.
